intro: Chamaecrista tenuisepala, also known as the slender-spurred cassia, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. The slender-spurred cassia has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in African and Asian cultures, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Chamaecrista tenuisepala include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a decoction, and is also dried and used in traditional herbal remedies.
